title: Getting Started with Virometrics
description: Learn the basics of the Virometrics platform

steps.title: Welcome to Virometrics
steps.description:
Virometrics is a comprehensive bioinformatics platform that streamlines your sequence analysis workflow.

In this tutorial, you'll learn how to load data, run tools, and export results.
steps.tip: You can navigate between steps using the Previous and Next buttons, or use the arrow keys on your keyboard.

steps.title: Loading Sequence Data
steps.description:
The first step in any analysis is loading your sequence data. Virometrics supports FASTA, FASTQ, and other common formats.

Click the Run Demo button below to simulate loading a FASTA file.
steps.onInput: function(value) { return value ? 'Validating: ' + value : 'Enter a file path'; }
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Loaded ' + value + ' successfully!' : 'Please enter a file path'; }

steps.title: Selecting Analysis Tools
steps.description:
Virometrics provides a rich collection of bioinformatics tools for sequence analysis, alignment, variant calling, and more.

Browse the available tools in the Tools section to see what's available.
steps.tip: Tools are organized by category: Assembly, Alignment, Variant Calling, Quality Control, and Export.

steps.title: Configuring Parameters
steps.description:
Each tool has configurable parameters that control its behavior. Common parameters include:

  • Quality threshold for filtering
  • Expected insert size for paired-end data
  • Number of threads for parallel processing
  • Output format preferences
steps.tip: Use sensible defaults for most parameters. Only change settings if you have specific requirements.

steps.title: Running the Analysis
steps.description:
Once configured, click the Run button to start your analysis. The execution will be tracked in real-time.

You can monitor progress through the Execution History panel.
steps.onExecute: function() { return 'Analysis started! Monitor progress in Execution History.'; }
steps.tip: Long-running analyses can be paused and resumed. Checkpoints are automatically saved.

steps.title: Viewing Results
steps.description:
Results are automatically saved to your workspace. Browse them in the Data section.

You can view quality reports, alignment statistics, and variant calls.
steps.tip: Results are organized by execution, making it easy to compare different runs.

steps.title: Exporting Results
steps.description:
Export your results in various formats suitable for publication or downstream analysis:

  • VCF for variants
  • FASTA/FASTQ for sequences
  • JSON for programmatic access
  • HTML reports for sharing
steps.tip: Exported files are compressed by default to save space.
